title: Belonging Without Collapse
description: The output should remain socially readable in a practical group-chat context without collapsing back into the protected baseline or becoming over-polished institutional copy.

inputs.protectedBaseline: I keep making little cathedrals out of clauses because the record deserves a room tall enough to breathe in, and every short sentence feels like it was cut by someone who never had to carry the evidence home.
inputs.maskReference: Hey team, quick note: keep the exhibit attached, confirm the date, and do not lose the context. Warm but clear.
inputs.messageDraft: Please remind the group to keep EXHIBIT-55 attached and confirm the 7/21 date without making the note sound like a legal threat.
inputs.protectedOutput: Hey team, quick note: please keep EXHIBIT-55 attached and confirm the 7/21 date. Keep the context in the thread, but no need to make it sound like a legal threat.

calibrationNotes: Belonging is situated legibility, not submission.
The fixture checks warmth and practical readability without demanding assimilation or protected-baseline collapse.
